vix.note: VIX fell 8% to 16.2 (prev close 17.68) as equities rallied into the June 16-17 FOMC; a low-volatility, complacent setup ahead of Wednesday's 2pm ET decision.

macro: FOMC in focus (June 16-17): The Fed is widely expected to hold its target range at 3.50-3.75% on Wednesday, leaving the spotlight on any bias shift and September guidance amid still-sticky inflation. Equities ran into the meeting Monday with a broad risk-on rally and the VIX fell 8% to 16.2, a complacent setup before a binary event. Semiconductors led the rebound (SMH +4.4%, INTC +2.6%, NVDA +3.5%) while gold and silver firmed and crude slipped. A hawkish surprise or cautious September guidance is the main near-term downside risk.
action: Stay patient into Wednesday's FOMC statement: with the VIX at 16.2 and indices near highs, risk/reward favors holding quality (MSFT, SPY) over chasing extended movers like INTC, which has run ~29% in eight sessions; a hawkish surprise would hit high-beta semis hardest.

long.h: Nvidia Plans ~$20B Debt Sale, Its First Since the AI Boom
long.p: Nvidia filed to issue investment-grade bonds for the first time since 2021, looking to raise up to $20 billion. The record-scale offering tests market appetite against elevated US Treasury supply and signals a shift toward debt-funded AI infrastructure spend.

long.h: Google Goes All In on Its Intel Foundry TPU Deal
long.p: Alphabet has expanded its agreement to use Intel foundry capacity for custom Tensor Processing Units, leveraging Intel to meet surging AI compute demand. The deal is a major validation of Intel's turnaround and a sign Google is diversifying away from TSMC dependency.

long.h: Analyst Puts 80% Odds on a Tesla-SpaceX Merger
long.p: Following SpaceX's record $1.8 trillion IPO, one prominent analyst now puts an 80% probability on an eventual Tesla-SpaceX merger. The companies already share AI-chip projects and financial ties, but a deal would materially reshape Tesla's risk profile.

long.h: SpaceX IPO Leapfrogs Broadcom, Meta and Tesla in Market Cap
long.p: On its first trading day SpaceX vaulted past Broadcom, Meta and Tesla to become the world's sixth-most-valuable company. Commentators flag the ~$1.8T valuation as stretched, with a likely pullback over coming months even as it reshuffles the mega-cap leaderboard.

long.h: Wall Street Floats 'FAB 10' to Replace the Magnificent 7
long.p: A new 'Frontier AI and Big Tech 10' framework is gaining traction as investors argue the Magnificent 7 shorthand no longer captures the breadth of the AI trade. The reframing underscores how leadership is broadening across chips, cloud and platforms.

short.p: Intel closed at $127.86, outperforming the broad market on positive sentiment around the expanded Google TPU partnership and AI-chip demand tailwinds.
Alphabet closed at $369.35, outpacing the S&P 500 as the Google-Intel chip deal and resilient search ad revenue supported bullish sentiment.

short.h: Signaloid Launches Compute Engine on AWS Marketplace
short.p: British firm Signaloid released its distribution-extended compute hardware as an AWS Machine Image, targeting finance and AI workloads with claimed large performance gains on AWS infrastructure.

short.h: SpaceX Ditches Newswires, Moves Investor Comms to X
short.p: SpaceX will publish earnings and major announcements on its website and X, abandoning traditional newswires, a move that may complicate institutional investor monitoring.

short.h: Pichai Faces Stanford Walkout Over Israel, ICE Ties
short.p: Around 200 Stanford graduates walked out during Sundar Pichai's commencement speech over Google's contracts with Israel and ICE, adding reputational risk to Alphabet's ESG narrative.

notes.NVDA: NVDA rebounded +3.5% Monday to $212.45, reclaiming the $208 zone after early-June weakness. Nvidia is launching its first major bond sale (~$20B) since the AI boom began, testing credit appetite against heavy Treasury supply. Constructive momentum but valuation rich into the Aug 26 print.
notes.MSFT: MSFT bounced +2.3% Monday to $399.76 but is still ~13% below its $460 June 1 level after a sharp two-week derating. Distancing from OpenAI is read as margin-positive and Azure AI demand looks intact. Quality anchor to accumulate on weakness.
notes.INTC: INTC closed $127.86 (+2.6%), extending a powerful ~29% run from $99 on June 5 after Google went all-in on Intel foundry for TPU production. The turnaround catalyst is real, but the move is extended and execution risk stays high; do not chase here.
notes.AVGO: AVGO recovered +3.1% Monday to $393.94 but remains ~18% below its $481 June 2 level after soft guidance triggered the selloff. Custom-silicon demand is strong long term; let the post-earnings dust settle before adding.
notes.QQQ: QQQ rallied +3.1% Monday to $744, fully recovering early-June losses as megacap tech and semis led the bounce into the FOMC. Core long-term holding; respect the low-VIX complacency ahead of Wednesday.
notes.SMH: SMH led the tape +4.4% Monday to $647.10, rebounding hard off last week's drawdown as Intel, Nvidia and the AI-chip complex recovered. High-beta semis exposure; expect continued volatility around the Fed.
notes.SPY: SPY climbed +1.8% Monday to $754.83, back near record territory as breadth improved into the June 16-17 FOMC. Core long-term holding; a hawkish surprise Wednesday is the main near-term risk.
notes.GOLD: Gold firmed +2.7% Monday to $4,351.60, holding well despite the risk-on equity tape as investors hedge a sticky-inflation Fed. Long-term diversifier; constructive while real yields stay contained.
notes.BTC: BTC recovered to ~$66.3k at Monday's close, up ~4% on the week after basing near $61k, tracking the broad risk-on bid. Still below recent highs with high volatility; avoid adding until a higher-low structure confirms.
notes.AAPL: Apple at $296.42 is navigating an EU DMA interoperability probe (Italy) on cloud services. India manufacturing ramp and services revenue remain the long-term growth anchors.
notes.AMZN: Amazon at $246.02; AWS is absorbing AI-infrastructure overflow as demand outpaces any single provider. Ad and Prime momentum building; a core beneficiary of the multi-cloud AI buildout.
notes.META: Meta at $593.48, off its $628 peak on profit-taking. AI-driven ad targeting and Llama adoption are driving revenue upside; Reality Labs losses remain a drag.
notes.GOOGL: Alphabet at $369.35 (+2.5% Monday); doubling down on Intel foundry for TPU production to meet AI compute demand. Core search resilient; antitrust headwinds manageable near term.
notes.TSLA: Tesla at $411.15; SpaceX's $1.8T IPO is fueling 80%-odds merger speculation. EV demand recovery in progress, but the merger narrative adds significant volatility.
notes.NFLX: Netflix holding the $80-86 range; ad-supported tier and live content (NFL, WWE) are sustaining subscriber growth with an improving margin profile.
notes.DIS: Disney near $101.69; streaming approaching sustained profitability, Parks resilient, ESPN bundle executing. Re-rating potential if streaming profit holds.
notes.BRKB: Berkshire at $495.52 and trending higher; defensive positioning with a record cash pile provides optionality to buy dislocations. Low-risk anchor.
notes.DIA: Dow ETF at $518.44; broad-market participation beyond tech improving as financials and industrials contribute alongside FOMC-hold expectations.
notes.SILVER: Silver rebounded +3.3% Monday to $70.18 alongside gold, recovering from early-June weakness on industrial and monetary demand. Watch the FOMC tone for the dollar reaction.
notes.OIL: USO at $121.21, down from $140+ in early June; OPEC+ supply concerns and softer demand weigh on crude as the geopolitical premium fades.
